UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT INNOCENTI LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUDULENT CONVEYANCE. SEE 18, 21, 22 AND 24. REASONABLENESS OF THE VALUE PAID FOR THE PROPERTY IS AN ISSUE OF FACT. BANKRUPTCY STAY DOES NOT EXTEND TO THE MOVING DEFENDANT. AUTHORITIES RELIED UPON BY BANKRUPTCY COURT AND CONSIDERATIONS TAKEN INTO ACCOUNT ARE NOT APPLICABLE TO THE CASE AT BAR. SEE FEDERAL LIFE INSURANCE CO. V. FIRST FINANCIAL GROUP OF TEXAS, INC (1980) 3 B.R. 375. JUDGE HARRY DORFMAN, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

George Lee, Esq. of Seiler Epstein (415-979-0500) is present for the plaintiff/opposing party

Court hears arguments from both sides. Court rules now as follows:
DEFENDANT NORTH BEACH PARTNERS' Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Verified Responses To Written Discovery is GRANTED. Plaintiff failed to serve timely responses. See CCP 2030.290. Defendant is awarded attorney's fees in the amount of $935. Written order is signed and filed.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

Ike M. Kaludi, Jr., Esq., Attorney-At-Law, (510-559-0320), for Inocenti, Defendant and moving party.

Stipulation and Order to continue hearing on Defendant's motion to expunge lis pendens is signed in open court. Defendant Innocenti's Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And Award Of Attorneys' Fees And Cost is continued to October 15, 2013 at 9:30am in Dept. 501 subject to further Order of the Court. Judge Peter Busch, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT INNOCENTI LLC's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges cause of action for fraudulent conveyance. See 18, 21, 22 and 24. Reasonableness of the value paid for the property is an issue of fact. Bankruptcy Stay does not extend to the moving defendant. Authorities relied upon by Bankruptcy Court and considerations taken into account are not applicable to the case at bar. See Federal Life Insurance Co. v. First Financial Group of Texas, Inc (1980) 3 B.R. 375. Judge Harry Dorfman, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
